title: erica coccinea subsp. uniflora
intro: Erica coccinea subsp. uniflora, also known as the single-flowered bell heather, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Erica coccinea subsp. uniflora include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain pathogens. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate various health conditions.
